# `nestedMinimal` Function

Remove null, empty string, and empty collection leaf entries from a nested map

Collapses a nested map to its leaf entries, removes null values, empty strings, and empty collections, then expands back to the original structure.

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
nestedMinimal(input dynamic, separator string...) dynamic
```

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `input` (Dynamic) The nested map or object to process.
<!-- variadic argument generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `separator` (Variadic, String) Separator used when collapsing and expanding path keys. Defaults to provider separator.

## Example Usage

```console
> provider::util::nestedMinimal({ dev = { api = { port = 8080 }, cache = {} }, prod = { api = { port = 8080 }, db = null } })
tomap({
  dev  = tomap({ api = tomap({ port = 8080 }) })
  prod = tomap({ api = tomap({ port = 8080 }) })
})
```

~> **Note:** `nestedMinimal` removes both null values and empty collections at any depth. Use `nestedCompact` if you only want to strip nulls.

### HCL Example

```terraform
locals {
  services = {
    dev  = { api = { port = 8080 }, web = null, cache = {} }
    prod = { api = { port = 8080 }, web = { port = 443 }, cache = {} }
  }
}

# Returns services with null and empty entries removed, preserving the nested structure
output "minimal_services" {
  value = provider::util::nestedMinimal(local.services)
}
```
